scenarios.description: Empty input yields no iterations so startDocument and endDocument are called without rule handling (iter.hasNext false)
scenarios.precondition: Use a Reader over empty CSS content so scan(reader,systemID,err) returns iterator with hasNext(false)
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.startDocument() called once; doc.endDocument() called once; no calls to handleAtRule or handleRuleSet

scenarios.description: Single at-rule token causes one handleAtRule invocation and then terminates the loop (iter.hasNext true then false, token.type==ATKEYWORD)
scenarios.precondition: Use a Reader over CSS with one at-rule (e.g. "@media {};"), real scan and stub CssContentHandler record calls
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.startDocument() once; handleAtRule invoked once; doc.endDocument() once; doc.startAtRule and doc.endAtRule called with rule name 'media'

scenarios.description: Single rule-set token triggers handleRuleSet once and then terminates (iter.hasNext true then false, token.type!=ATKEYWORD)
scenarios.precondition: Use a Reader over CSS with one ruleset (e.g. "h1{}"), real scan and stub handlers record selectors and declarations
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.startDocument() once; doc.selectors called with selector for 'h1'; doc.endSelectors called once; doc.endDocument() once

scenarios.description: PrematureEOFException in handleRuleSet breaks the loop and calls endDocument (iter.next throws PrematureEOFException)
scenarios.precondition: Subclass CssParser overriding scan(...) to return a stub CssTokenIterator that hasNext() returns true then next() throws PrematureEOFException; handlers record calls
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.startDocument() once; no further handler calls; doc.endDocument() once; no exception escapes
pseudocode: // stub scan returns iterator that throws PrematureEOFException on next()
